violations.text: Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (syrup) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

The flow pressure of the hot water sanitizing rinse (PSI) for both dishwashers was not between 15-25 PSI as required. PIC to use 3 bay sink to sanitize until dishwashers are serviced and reading 15-25 PSI. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

The following were in poor repair: 1) Hot-hold unit door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. 2) Inspector observed dripping condensate from refrigerator #3 and #4. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt or other debris: 1. Standing fans (warewashing area) 2. Mixer New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026
The wall behind the three-bay sink area is in poor repair, paint was observed chipping. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026
A soap dispenser was observed at the food prep sink near the vacant serving line. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the spray bottles was less than 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200-400 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025
The faucet at the sink near the dishwasher was observed to be leaking. A plumbing system shall be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Canned Tomato Sauce were only heated to 89 to 110 degrees Fahrenheit in hot holding unit. Ready to eat foods taken from a commercially processed can or intact package from a food processing plant must be heated to at least 135 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Based on time and temperature observed, the pans of Sauce were moved to the ovens for rapid reheating at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of the PIC, large plastic tubs of previously prepared salads (lettuce, tomato, carrot) was placed covered in the walk-in refrigerator. Large containers of salads were moved to the walk-in freezer for rapid cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2024
